sections.section_title: Compensating Controls for PCI DSS Requirements

policies.policy_description: Establishes requirements for ongoing compliance with the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) to protect cardholder data.
policies.policy_text: This policy outlines the three ongoing steps for adhering to the PCI DSS: Assess, Repair, and Report. Organizations must identify all locations of cardholder data, take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, analyze them for vulnerabilities, fix identified vulnerabilities, securely remove unnecessary cardholder data storage, and document assessment and remediation details.
policies.scope: This policy applies to all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data, including merchants, payment processors, acquirers, issuers, and service providers.
policies.objective: The primary objective of this policy is to ensure the protection of cardholder data through continuous compliance with PCI DSS requirements, thereby minimizing the risk of data breaches and ensuring the integrity of payment card transactions.
policies.policy_subcategory: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Organizations must fix identified vulnerabilities and securely remove unnecessary cardholder data storage.
subpolicies.subpolicy_text: This subpolicy requires organizations to address vulnerabilities found during assessments and to securely remove any unnecessary cardholder data storage.

control.WHAT: Fix identified vulnerabilities and remove unnecessary cardholder data.
control.WHO: IT Security team and Data Management team.
control.WHEN: Immediately upon identification of vulnerabilities and during regular data reviews.
control.HOW: Implement patches, updates, and secure data deletion methods.
control.WHERE: All systems and databases that store cardholder data.

compliances.SectionTitle: Compensating Controls for PCI DSS Requirements
compliances.Description: Organizations must implement a systematic approach to identify, fix, and document vulnerabilities in systems that store or process cardholder data, ensuring unnecessary cardholder data is securely removed.
compliances.Notes: AI-generated from Compensating Controls for PCI DSS Requirements - PCI DSS Compliance Policy
compliances.Scope: All systems and databases that store or process cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure that all identified vulnerabilities are addressed promptly to protect cardholder data from unauthorized access and breaches.
compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to address vulnerabilities leading to data breaches, unauthorized access to sensitive cardholder data.

risk_details.RiskTitle: Data Breach Due to Unpatched Vulnerabilities
risk_details.PossibleDamage: Exposure of sensitive cardholder data, regulatory fines, loss of customer trust.
risk_details.BusinessImpact: Significant impact on customer trust and potential financial penalties from regulatory bodies.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Unpatched vulnerabilities in systems that process cardholder data can lead to unauthorized access and data breaches, resulting in severe financial and reputational damage.

RiskMitigation.1: Implement a robust vulnerability management program.
RiskMitigation.2: Regularly review and update security controls.
RiskMitigation.3: Train staff on data protection and security best practices.

compliances.SectionTitle: Compensating Controls for PCI DSS Requirements
compliances.Description: Organizations must ensure that unnecessary cardholder data is securely deleted using approved methods to prevent unauthorized recovery.
compliances.Notes: AI-generated from Compensating Controls for PCI DSS Requirements - PCI DSS Compliance Policy
compliances.Scope: All systems and databases that store cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To mitigate the risk of data breaches by ensuring that unnecessary cardholder data is permanently removed from systems.
compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to securely delete cardholder data leading to data recovery and unauthorized access.

risk_details.RiskTitle: Unauthorized Recovery of Deleted Cardholder Data
risk_details.PossibleDamage: Regulatory fines, loss of customer trust, legal liabilities.
risk_details.BusinessImpact: Severe impact on compliance standing and potential legal repercussions.
risk_details.RiskDescription: If cardholder data is not securely deleted, it may be recovered by unauthorized individuals, leading to data breaches and regulatory violations.

RiskMitigation.2: Regularly train staff on data handling and deletion procedures.
RiskMitigation.3: Conduct audits to ensure compliance with data deletion policies.

policies.policy_description: Establishes requirements for ongoing compliance with the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) to protect cardholder data.
policies.policy_text: This policy outlines the three ongoing steps for adhering to the PCI DSS: Assess, Repair, and Report. Organizations must identify all locations of cardholder data, take an inventory of IT assets and business processes for payment card processing, analyze them for vulnerabilities, fix identified vulnerabilities, securely remove unnecessary cardholder data storage, and document assessment and remediation details.
policies.scope: This policy applies to all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data, including merchants, payment processors, acquirers, issuers, and service providers.
policies.objective: The primary objective of this policy is to ensure continuous compliance with PCI DSS requirements, thereby protecting cardholder data and minimizing the risk of data breaches.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Organizations must document assessment and remediation details and submit compliance reports to relevant entities.
subpolicies.subpolicy_text: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for reporting compliance status to acquiring banks and card brands.

control.WHAT: Document assessment and remediation details and submit compliance reports.
control.WHEN: Quarterly and upon request from acquiring banks or card brands.
control.HOW: Prepare compliance reports and maintain documentation of assessments.
control.WHERE: All relevant compliance reporting systems and documentation repositories.

compliances.Description: Organizations must document assessment and remediation details and submit compliance reports to relevant entities on a quarterly basis.
compliances.Notes: AI-generated from Choosing a Qualified Security Assessor - PCI DSS Compliance Process Policy
compliances.Scope: All systems and applications that store or process cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely and accurate reporting of compliance status to maintain adherence to PCI DSS requirements.
compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inaccurate reporting leading to regulatory fines, loss of customer trust, and potential legal action.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Compliance and Legal departments may face scrutiny and penalties.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Regulatory fines, loss of business reputation, potential legal consequences.
risk_details.BusinessImpact: Legal and compliance teams may incur additional costs and resources to address compliance failures.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Failure to accurately document and report compliance status can lead to significant regulatory penalties and damage to the organization's reputation.

RiskMitigation.2: Implement a review process for compliance reports before submission.
RiskMitigation.3: Engage external consultants for compliance assessments.

compliances.Description: Organizations must maintain detailed records of assessment and remediation efforts related to PCI DSS compliance.
compliances.Notes: AI-generated from Choosing a Qualified Security Assessor - PCI DSS Compliance Process Policy
compliances.Scope: All compliance-related documentation and remediation activities.
compliances.Objective: Ensure that all remediation efforts are documented to provide evidence of compliance and facilitate audits.
compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inability to provide documentation during audits leading to compliance failures and penalties.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Compliance and Audit teams may face challenges in demonstrating adherence to PCI DSS.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Failed audits, regulatory fines, increased operational costs.
risk_details.BusinessImpact: Compliance teams may need to allocate additional resources to rectify documentation issues.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Lack of thorough documentation of remediation efforts can lead to compliance failures and increased scrutiny from regulators.

RiskMitigation.1: Conduct regular training on documentation standards.
RiskMitigation.2: Implement a checklist for remediation documentation.
RiskMitigation.3: Schedule periodic reviews of documentation practices.

compliances.Description: Organizations must submit compliance reports to acquiring banks or card brands upon request.
compliances.Notes: AI-generated from Choosing a Qualified Security Assessor - PCI DSS Compliance Process Policy
compliances.Scope: All relevant compliance reporting systems and documentation repositories.
compliances.Objective: Ensure that compliance reports are readily available and submitted in a timely manner to maintain PCI DSS compliance.
compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Delayed or missing compliance reports leading to regulatory penalties and loss of business relationships.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Compliance and Management teams may face increased scrutiny and operational challenges.

risk_details.PossibleDamage: Loss of merchant status, regulatory fines, damage to business relationships.
risk_details.BusinessImpact: Compliance teams may incur additional costs and resources to address compliance failures.
risk_details.RiskDescription: Failure to submit compliance reports in a timely manner can lead to significant regulatory penalties and damage to the organization's reputation.

RiskMitigation.2: Designate a compliance officer to oversee report submissions.
